title: Insurance basics
description: Practice your understanding of insurance terminology

assessment_items.question_content:
Understanding insurance terms helps you know how your insurance keeps you safe and what it pays for.

**What is a *premium* in the context of insurance?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the maximum amount the insurance company will pay for a claim
the amount the insured pays to the insurance company in exchange for coverage
the amount the insured must pay out of pocket before the insurance company will pay for a claim

assessment_items.question_content:
Understanding insurance terms helps you know what you're paying for and what's covered. 

**What does the term *premium* in insurance mean?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the money you get when you make a claim
the money you pay regularly to keep your insurance active
the maximum amount your insurance will cover

assessment_items.question_content:
It is important to understand insurance terminology so that you know what you are paying for, what your policy covers, and what your responsibilities are as the insured.

**What is the meaning of a *premium* in insurance?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a fixed amount the insured pays each time they receive a service that is covered by their insurance policy
the amount the insured pays to the insurance company in order to  get coverage
the amount the insured pays out of pocket before the insurance company will pay for a claim

assessment_items.question_content:
When you purchase an insurance policy, it will list an amount you must pay out of pocket before the insurance company will pay for a claim.

**What is this amount called?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a deductible
a co-pay
a policy limit

assessment_items.question_content:
When you buy an insurance policy, there is a term that refers to the amount you must pay before the insurance company will step in.

**What is this term called?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a deductible
a premium
a policy limit

assessment_items.question_content:
When you buy an insurance policy, the company will tell you how much you need to pay on your own before the insurance company will cover costs.

**What is this part of the policy called?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a deductible
a premium
a policy limit

assessment_items.question_content:
Understanding what terms like  **deductible**, **co-pay**, and **policy limit** mean can help you understand what you're paying for and what you're covered for. 

**What is the meaning of *co-pay* in insurance?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the maximum amount the insurance company will pay for a claim
an amount the insured must pay each time they receive a service that is covered by their insurance policy
the amount the insured must pay out of pocket before the insurance company will pay for a claim

assessment_items.question_content:
Understanding what terms like  **deductible**, **co-pay**, and **policy limit** mean can help you understand what you're paying for and what you're covered for. 

**What is a  *co-pay*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a fixed amount the insured must pay each time they receive a service that is covered by their insurance policy
the amount the insured pays to the insurance company in exchange for coverage
the amount the insured must pay out of pocket before the insurance company will pay for a claim

assessment_items.question_content:
Understanding what terms like  **deductible**, **co-pay**, and **policy limit** mean can help you understand what you're paying for and what you're covered for. 

**What is the meaning of *co-pay* in insurance?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the maximum amount the insurance company will pay for a claim
an amount the insured must pay each time they receive a service that is covered by their insurance policy
the amount the insured pays to the insurance company in order to  get coverage

assessment_items.question_content:
When you purchase an insurance policy, there is a term that refers to the maximum amount the insurance company will pay for a claim.

**What is this term called?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a deductible
a premium
a policy limit

assessment_items.question_content:
When you purchase an insurance policy, you will see a term that lays out the highest amount the insurance company will pay for a claim.

**What is this term called?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a deductible
a co-pay
a policy limit

assessment_items.question_content:
When you purchase an insurance policy, you will see a term that defines the most money the insurance company is willing to pay in the event of a claim.

**What is this term called?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a premium
a co-pay
a policy limit
